_**User (2026-03-19 04:28:58Z)**_

```
# 1. Define the Volume
model_vol = modal.Volume.from_name("llm-weights-vol", create_if_missing=True)

# 2. Add it to your Sandbox/Function
sandbox = modal.Sandbox.create(
    # ... other config ...
    volumes={"/root/models": model_vol},
    # Ensure your HuggingFace cache points here!
    secrets=[modal.Secret.from_dict({"HF_HOME": "/root/models"})] 
) 
```

Will this load the models onto the volume? Preventing the need to download the model each time?

---

_**Agent (copilot/gpt-5.3-codex)**_

Short answer: not by itself, but yes for caching behavior.

What your snippet does:
1. Mounts a persistent Modal Volume at /root/models.
2. Points Hugging Face cache there via HF_HOME.

Result:
1. First run: models are still downloaded once.
2. Later runs: the same models are reused from the volume, so you avoid re-downloading.

Important caveats:
1. This does not pre-load models automatically. It only changes where downloads are stored.
2. In your current script, you already pass secrets with dotenv_secret, so include both secrets together (not one replacing the other).
3. Some libraries also look at TRANSFORMERS_CACHE and HUGGINGFACE_HUB_CACHE, so setting those to paths under /root/models can make caching more reliable across tools.

If you want true no-download startup, do a one-time warmup job that explicitly downloads the model into that volume first.
